description_long: This is a wall-mounted stone fountain with a semi-circular arched top and a recessed niche, featuring a scallop shell motif carved into the upper section. The fountain's structure is robust, with thick stone sides and a slightly protruding lower basin area. At the center of the niche, there is a decorative metal spout, likely made of bronze or copper, from which water would typically flow. The stone surface shows signs of weathering and patina, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The overall design is classical, with simple yet elegant lines and a focus on symmetry. The fountain is intended to be placed against a vertical surface, such as a wall in a public square or park.
description: A classical wall-mounted stone fountain with an arched top, scallop shell motif, and central metal spout. The design is robust and symmetrical, with a recessed niche and weathered stone surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched top, scallop shell motif, recessed niche, and central metal spout.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the niche and the protruding lower basin, with the arched top visible in profile.
description_view_2: Alternate front view, similar to view 0, highlighting the symmetry and central placement of the spout.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth of the niche and the curvature of the basin.
attribute_ornamentation_details: The fountain features a prominent scallop shell motif carved into the upper niche, adding a classical decorative touch. The arched top and symmetrical stonework provide a sense of elegance and formality. The metal spout at the center is simple but functional, with a patina that suggests age.
attribute_basin_structure: The basin is semi-circular and recessed, forming a shallow bowl at the base of the niche. It is integrated into the stone structure, with a smooth rim and no visible tiering or compartments.
attribute_jet_or_spray_pattern: A single central spout is present, likely designed to produce a gentle stream of water flowing downward into the basin. No additional jets or spray patterns are visible.
